name: Onyx Eight
explanation: A bright, punchy 8-voice analog polysynth in the Oberheim OB-8 lineage. Sister to the warm 2-pole Voice Eight but steeper and brighter: each voice runs a sawtooth plus a pulse whose width is swept by a per-patch PWM LFO, through a cascaded FOUR-POLE resonant low-pass with its own envelope, and a unison Spread detunes the oscillators for the classic brassy OB pad. Controls: Cutoff, Resonance, Env Amount, PWM (pulse-width sweep depth), Spread (unison detune) and Level.
